name: JSDoc (JavaScript Documentation Generator)

performance.notes: Generation time proportional to codebase size

agent_readiness.notes: JavaScript documentation generator from JSDoc comment blocks. /** @param {string} name - User name @returns {string} Greeting */. function greet(name) { return `Hello ${name}` }. npx jsdoc src/ -r -d docs. jsdoc.json config: { source: { include: ['src'] }, opts: { destination: 'docs', recurse: true } }. Tags: @param, @returns, @type, @typedef, @property, @callback, @async, @example, @see, @since, @deprecated, @throws, @module, @class, @memberof, @private, @public, @protected. TypeScript: use @ts-check with JSDoc types for type checking without .ts files. Templates: default, better-docs, docdash, clean-jsdoc-theme. Integration with TypeDoc for TS. Generates HTML documentation site. Used by many JS projects (jQuery, etc).

gotchas: TypeScript projects should use TypeDoc instead
Configuration can be verbose for large projects
@type annotations in JSDoc are less ergonomic than TypeScript types
best_when: Pure JavaScript projects (not TypeScript) needing API documentation
Legacy jQuery-style projects with JSDoc comments already written
When @ts-check with JSDoc types is used as TypeScript alternative
avoid_when: Modern documentation sites needing MDX, search, versioning (use Docusaurus)
summary: JSDoc generates HTML API documentation from /** */ comment blocks. @param, @returns, @type, @example, @deprecated. npx jsdoc src/ -r -d docs. Apache-2.0. Best for pure JavaScript codebases. TypeScript projects should use TypeDoc. Default theme outdated — use docdash or clean-jsdoc-theme. @ts-check lets JSDoc annotations provide type checking in JS files.
